compression.description: Data compression method. One of `` (for no compression), `gzip`, `bzip2` or `snappy`.
compression.title: compression

encryption.description: Whether to instruct the storage provider to encrypt data files. One of `` (use the storage container default), `AES256` or `aws:kms`.
encryption.title: encryption

jobs.description: Number of data files to be archived or restored in parallel.
jobs.title: jobs

destinationPath.description:
Overrides the provider specific default path. Defaults to:
S3: s3://<bucket><path>
Azure: https://<storageAccount>.<serviceName>.core.windows.net/<containerName><path>
Google: gs://<bucket><path>
destinationPath.title: destinationPath

enabled.description: You need to configure backups manually, so backups are disabled by default.
enabled.title: enabled

endpointCA.description: Specifies a CA bundle to validate a privately signed certificate.

create.description: Creates a secret with the given value if true, otherwise uses an existing secret.
create.title: create

endpointCA.title: endpointCA

endpointURL.description:
Overrides the provider specific default endpoint. Defaults to:
S3: https://s3.<region>.amazonaws.com"
endpointURL.title: endpointURL

inheritFromIAMRole.description: Use the role based authentication without providing explicitly the keys
inheritFromIAMRole.title: inheritFromIAMRole

create.description: Whether to create a secret for the backup credentials
create.title: create

name.description: Name of the backup credentials secret
name.title: name

compression.description: WAL compression method. One of `` (for no compression), `gzip`, `bzip2` or `snappy`.
compression.title: compression

encryption.description: Whether to instruct the storage provider to encrypt WAL files. One of `` (use the storage container default), `AES256` or `aws:kms`.
encryption.title: encryption

maxParallel.description: Number of WAL files to be archived or restored in parallel.
maxParallel.title: maxParallel

certificates.description:
The configuration for the CA and related certificates.
See: https://cloudnative-pg.io/documentation/current/cloudnative-pg.v1/#postgresql-cnpg-io-v1-CertificatesConfiguration
certificates.title: certificates

enabled.description: Deploys a console StatefulSet to run long-running commands against the cluster (e.g. `CREATE INDEX`).
enabled.title: enabled

enablePDB.description:
Allow to disable PDB, mainly useful for upgrade of single-instance clusters or development purposes
See: https://cloudnative-pg.io/documentation/current/kubernetes_upgrade/#pod-disruption-budgets
enablePDB.title: enablePDB

enableSuperuserAccess.description:
When this option is enabled, the operator will use the SuperuserSecret to update the postgres user password.
If the secret is not present, the operator will automatically create one.
When this option is disabled, the operator will ignore the SuperuserSecret content, delete it when automatically created,
and then blank the password of the postgres user by setting it to NULL.
enableSuperuserAccess.title: enableSuperuserAccess

env.description: Env follows the Env format to pass environment variables to the pods created in the cluster
env.title: env

envFrom.description: EnvFrom follows the EnvFrom format to pass environment variables sources to the pods to be used by Env
envFrom.title: envFrom

imageCatalogRef.description: Reference to `ImageCatalog` of `ClusterImageCatalog`, if specified takes precedence over `cluster.imageName`
imageCatalogRef.title: imageCatalogRef

imageName.description:
Name of the container image, supporting both tags (<image>:<tag>) and digests for deterministic and repeatable deployments:
<image>:<tag>
imageName.title: imageName

imagePullPolicy.description:
Image pull policy. One of Always, Never or IfNotPresent. If not defined, it defaults to IfNotPresent. Cannot be updated.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images#updating-images
imagePullPolicy.title: imagePullPolicy

imagePullSecrets.description:
The list of pull secrets to be used to pull the images.
See: https://cloudnative-pg.io/documentation/current/cloudnative-pg.v1/#postgresql-cnpg-io-v1-LocalObjectReference
imagePullSecrets.title: imagePullSecrets

initdb.description:
BootstrapInitDB is the configuration of the bootstrap process when initdb is used.
See: https://cloudnative-pg.io/documentation/current/bootstrap/
See: https://cloudnative-pg.io/documentation/current/cloudnative-pg.v1/#postgresql-cnpg-io-v1-bootstrapinitdb
initdb.title: initdb

logLevel.description: The instances' log level, one of the following values: error, warning, info (default), debug, trace
logLevel.title: logLevel

customQueries.description:
Custom Prometheus metrics
Will be stored in the ConfigMap
customQueries.title: customQueries

customQueriesSecret.description:
- name: "pg_cache_hit_ratio"
   query: "SELECT current_database() as datname, sum(heap_blks_hit) / (sum(heap_blks_hit) + sum(heap_blks_read)) as ratio FROM pg_statio_user_tables;"
   target_databases: ["*"]
   predicate_query: "SELECT '{{ .Values.version.postgresql }}';"
   metrics:
     - datname:
         usage: "LABEL"
         description: "Name of the database"
     - ratio:
         usage: GAUGE
         description: "Cache hit ratio"
The list of secrets containing the custom queries
customQueriesSecret.title: customQueriesSecret

disableDefaultQueries.description:
Whether the default queries should be injected.
Set it to true if you don't want to inject default queries into the cluster.
disableDefaultQueries.title: disableDefaultQueries

labels.description:
Additional labels to set on the generated PodMonitor resource.
Add labels your monitoring stack requires (for example `team-name`).
labels.title: labels

metricRelabelings.description:
The list of metric relabelings for the PodMonitor.
Applied to samples before ingestion.
metricRelabelings.title: metricRelabelings

relabelings.description:
The list of relabelings for the PodMonitor.
Applied to samples before scraping.
relabelings.title: relabelings

enabled.description: Whether to enable the PrometheusRule automated alerts
enabled.title: enabled

podSecurityContext.description:
Configure the Pod Security Context.
See: https://cloudnative-pg.io/documentation/preview/security/
podSecurityContext.title: podSecurityContext

postgresGID.description: The GID of the postgres user inside the image, defaults to 26
postgresGID.title: postgresGID

postgresUID.description: The UID of the postgres user inside the image, defaults to 26
postgresUID.title: postgresUID

pg_hba.description:
method: any
number: 1
PostgreSQL Host Based Authentication rules (lines to be appended to the pg_hba.conf file)
pg_hba.title: pg_hba

pg_ident.description:
- host all all 10.244.0.0/16 md5
PostgreSQL User Name Maps rules (lines to be appended to the pg_ident.conf file)
pg_ident.title: pg_ident

shared_preload_libraries.description:
- mymap   /^(.*)
Lists of shared preload libraries to add to the default ones
shared_preload_libraries.title: shared_preload_libraries

primaryUpdateMethod.description:
Method to follow to upgrade the primary server during a rolling update procedure, after all replicas have been
successfully updated. It can be switchover (default) or restart.
primaryUpdateMethod.title: primaryUpdateMethod

primaryUpdateStrategy.description:
Strategy to follow to upgrade the primary server during a rolling update procedure, after all replicas have been
successfully updated: it can be automated (unsupervised - default) or manual (supervised)
primaryUpdateStrategy.title: primaryUpdateStrategy

resources.description:
Resources requirements of every generated Pod.
Please refer to https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/ for more information.
We strongly advise you use the same setting for limits and requests so that your cluster pods are given a Guaranteed QoS.
See: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-qos/
resources.title: resources

roles.description:
This feature enables declarative management of existing roles, as well as the creation of new roles if they are not
already present in the database.
See: https://cloudnative-pg.io/documentation/current/declarative_role_management/
roles.title: roles

serviceAccountTemplate.description: Configure the metadata of the generated service account
serviceAccountTemplate.title: serviceAccountTemplate

services.description: Customization of service definitions. Please refer to https://cloudnative-pg.io/documentation/current/service_management/
services.title: services

fullnameOverride.description: Override the full name of the chart
fullnameOverride.title: fullnameOverride

global.description: Global values are values that can be accessed from any chart or subchart by exactly the same name.
global.title: global

create.description: Whether to provision an image catalog. If imageCatalog.images is empty this option will be ignored.
create.title: create

images.description: List of images to be provisioned in an image catalog.
images.title: images

mode.description:
##
Cluster mode of operation. Available modes:
* `standalone` - default mode. Creates new or updates an existing CNPG cluster.
* `replica` - Creates a replica cluster from an existing CNPG cluster.
* `recovery` - Same as standalone but creates a cluster from a backup, object store or via pg_basebackup.
mode.title: mode

nameOverride.description: Override the name of the chart
nameOverride.title: nameOverride

namespaceOverride.description: Override the namespace of the chart
namespaceOverride.title: namespaceOverride

clusterName.description:
#
The original cluster name when used in backups. Also known as serverName.
clusterName.title: clusterName

database.description: Name of the database used by the application. Default: `app`.
database.title: database

destinationPath.description:
Overrides the provider specific default path. Defaults to:
S3: s3://<bucket><path>
Azure: https://<storageAccount>.<serviceName>.core.windows.net/<containerName><path>
Google: gs://<bucket><path>
destinationPath.title: destinationPath

create.title: create

endpointCA.title: endpointCA

Overrides the provider specific default endpoint. Defaults to:
S3: https://s3.<region>.amazonaws.com"
Leave empty if using the default S3 endpoint
endpointURL.title: endpointURL

pgDumpExtraOptions.description:
List of custom options to pass to the `pg_dump` command. IMPORTANT: Use these options with caution and at your
own risk, as the operator does not validate their content. Be aware that certain options may conflict with the
operator's intended functionality or design.
pgDumpExtraOptions.title: pgDumpExtraOptions

pgRestoreExtraOptions.description:
List of custom options to pass to the `pg_restore` command. IMPORTANT: Use these options with caution and at
your own risk, as the operator does not validate their content. Be aware that certain options may conflict with the
operator's intended functionality or design.
pgRestoreExtraOptions.title: pgRestoreExtraOptions

postImportApplicationSQL.description:
List of SQL queries to be executed as a superuser in the application database right after is imported.
To be used with extreme care. Only available in microservice type.
postImportApplicationSQL.title: postImportApplicationSQL

schemaOnly.description: When set to true, only the pre-data and post-data sections of pg_restore are invoked, avoiding data import.
schemaOnly.title: schemaOnly

create.title: create

key.description: The key in the secret containing the password
key.title: key

name.description: Name of the secret containing the password
name.title: name

value.description: The password value to use when creating the secret
value.title: value

method.description:
#
Available recovery methods:
* `backup` - Recovers a CNPG cluster from a CNPG backup (PITR supported) Needs to be on the same cluster in the same namespace.
* `object_store` - Recovers a CNPG cluster from a barman object store (PITR supported).
* `pg_basebackup` - Recovers a CNPG cluster viaa streaming replication protocol. Useful if you want to
       migrate databases to CloudNativePG, even from outside Kubernetes.
* `import` - Import one or more databases from an existing Postgres cluster.
method.title: method
